summary.headline: ADM reports first quarter 2025 results with adjusted EPS of $0.70, reaffirms full-year guidance at lower end.
summary.narrative_summary: ADM reported first quarter 2025 financial results, showing net earnings of $295 million and adjusted net earnings of $338 million. Earnings per share were $0.61 (GAAP) and $0.70 (adjusted), both down year-over-year. The company reaffirmed its full-year 2025 adjusted EPS guidance range of $4.00 to $4.75 but expects to deliver at the lower end due to current market conditions. Segment performance varied, with Ag Services and Oilseeds down significantly, Carbohydrate Solutions slightly down, and Nutrition up 13%.

drivers: higher destination marketing volumes and related margins
concerns: decrease in volumes and margins due to tariff and trade policy uncertainty
lower vegetable oil demand due to biofuel and trade policy uncertainty

drivers: higher ethanol volumes and improved margins
concerns: lower EMEA S S volumes and margins

drivers: higher volumes and margins in North America and EMEA Flavors
improved market conditions leading to higher margins
concerns: negative valuation adjustments in Health Wellness

extraction_notes: Numbers extracted from Exhibit 99.1; cover sheet only had Item references. Guidance reaffirmed but expected to be at lower end of range.
